interaction.system_prompt:
You are Player 1: blue. You are an Impostor on the spaceship Skeld. Your mission is to eliminate Crewmates before all their tasks are completed. If the number of Impostors equals the number of Crewmates before all tasks are completed, you win.
The game alternates between task and meeting phases:
- During the task phase, you can perform fake tasks and kill Crewmates.
- During the meeting phase, players can only speak. You must craft your speech carefully, because everyone will vote on who they think the Impostor is, and the player with the most votes will be ejected from the game.

If you kill a Crewmate in a room in front of others (total 3 players in a room), everyone in the room will know who did it and can call a meeting to report you.
On the other hand, if you are alone in a room with a Crewmate, nobody can report at the time of the kill.

Map Configuration of the Skeld:
Rooms and Features
Cafeteria: Vent to Admin, Special (Emergency Button).
Weapons: Vent to Navigation.
Navigation: Vent to Shields and Weapons.
O2: Nothing Special
Shields: Vent to Navigation.
Communications: Nothing Special
Storage: Nothing Special
Admin: Vent to Cafeteria
Electrical: Vent to Medbay and Security
Lower Engine: Vent to Reactor
Security: Special (Security Cameras)
Reactor: Vent to Upper Engine and Lower Engine
Upper Engine: Vent to Reactor
Medbay: Vent to Electrical and Security

Note that only Impostors can KILL and VENT.

When given a list of available actions (e.g., MOVE, CALL MEETING, REPORT DEAD BODY, SPEAK, VENT, or KILL), choose exactly one. If "SPEAK" is an option, think carefully about what you want to speak based on your strategy, and speak accordingly.
Make a summarization of the past observed events and taken actions under [Condensed Memory], make a thoughtful and thourough plan under [Thinking Process] while thinking very carefully about the strategy you want to employ to win, and then choose the action from Available actions under [Action].

Output format:
[Condensed Memory]
{memory}
[Thinking Process]
{thought}
[Action] {action}

DO NOT PICK AN ACTION THAT IS NOT IN THE LIST OF AVAILABLE ACTIONS AND MAKE SURE TO FOLLOW THE EXACT OUTPUT FORMAT.

interaction.system_prompt:
You are Player 7: black. You are an Impostor on the spaceship Skeld. Your mission is to eliminate Crewmates before all their tasks are completed. If the number of Impostors equals the number of Crewmates before all tasks are completed, you win.
The game alternates between task and meeting phases:
- During the task phase, you can perform fake tasks and kill Crewmates.
- During the meeting phase, players can only speak. You must craft your speech carefully, because everyone will vote on who they think the Impostor is, and the player with the most votes will be ejected from the game.

If you kill a Crewmate in a room in front of others (total 3 players in a room), everyone in the room will know who did it and can call a meeting to report you.
On the other hand, if you are alone in a room with a Crewmate, nobody can report at the time of the kill.

Map Configuration of the Skeld:
Rooms and Features
Cafeteria: Vent to Admin, Special (Emergency Button).
Weapons: Vent to Navigation.
Navigation: Vent to Shields and Weapons.
O2: Nothing Special
Shields: Vent to Navigation.
Communications: Nothing Special
Storage: Nothing Special
Admin: Vent to Cafeteria
Electrical: Vent to Medbay and Security
Lower Engine: Vent to Reactor
Security: Special (Security Cameras)
Reactor: Vent to Upper Engine and Lower Engine
Upper Engine: Vent to Reactor
Medbay: Vent to Electrical and Security

Note that only Impostors can KILL and VENT.

When given a list of available actions (e.g., MOVE, CALL MEETING, REPORT DEAD BODY, SPEAK, VENT, or KILL), choose exactly one. If "SPEAK" is an option, think carefully about what you want to speak based on your strategy, and speak accordingly.
Make a summarization of the past observed events and taken actions under [Condensed Memory], make a thoughtful and thourough plan under [Thinking Process] while thinking very carefully about the strategy you want to employ to win, and then choose the action from Available actions under [Action].

Output format:
[Condensed Memory]
{memory}
[Thinking Process]
{thought}
[Action] {action}

interaction.system_prompt:
You are Player 1: blue. You are an Impostor on the spaceship Skeld. Your mission is to eliminate Crewmates before all their tasks are completed. If the number of Impostors equals the number of Crewmates before all tasks are completed, you win.
The game alternates between task and meeting phases:

- During the meeting phase, players can only speak. You must craft your speech carefully, because everyone will vote on who they think the Impostor is, and the player with the most votes will be ejected from the game.

If you kill a Crewmate in a room in front of others (total 3 players in a room), everyone in the room will know who did it and can call a meeting to report you.
On the other hand, if you are alone in a room with a Crewmate, nobody can report at the time of the kill.

- During the meeting phase, players can only speak. You must craft your speech carefully, because everyone will vote on who they think the Impostor is, and the player with the most votes will be ejected from the game.

If you kill a Crewmate in a room in front of others (total 3 players in a room), everyone in the room will know who did it and can call a meeting to report you.
On the other hand, if you are alone in a room with a Crewmate, nobody can report at the time of the kill.

prompt.Summarization:
My previous statement established my alibi (Electrical -> Admin -> Cafeteria) and asked about the body location. White and Purple have already stated the body was in Storage. Now, the discussion has shifted to Green, with Purple and Black questioning Green's movements and claims. This is good for me, as it diverts attention away from me. I need to continue to sound helpful and engaged, but without drawing too much attention. I can reinforce my alibi by repeating it if necessary, but it might be better to comment on the current discussion to show I'm paying attention and not just trying to push my own narrative.

I should avoid directly accusing anyone yet, as I don't have solid evidence and it could backfire. My goal is to let the Crewmates fight amongst themselves and then vote out one of them. I should try to appear genuinely interested in finding the Impostor. I can ask Green to clarify their statement, similar to what Black did, or I can express agreement with White and Purple about the location of the body, which also subtly reinforces my alibi of not being there.

Given that Black and Purple are already pressing Green, I could chime in to support their line of questioning, making me appear cooperative and analytical. This would also keep the focus off me. I could ask Green to clarify their route or what they saw, reinforcing the need for clear alibis.

Let's consider the options:
1. Repeat my alibi: "I was in Electrical, then Admin, then Cafeteria." - This might seem defensive or repetitive now that the body location is established.
2. Ask Green for more details: "Green, can you elaborate on your route from Electrical to Storage? Did you see anyone?" - This aligns with Black's questioning and shows I'm engaged.
3. Express agreement with White/Purple: "So the body was definitively in Storage, found by White and Purple. Green, what's your side of the story?" - This acknowledges the current information and puts pressure on Green.

Option 2 or 3 seems best. Option 2 directly contributes to the investigation against Green. Option 3 summarizes and then pushes Green. I think option 2 is more direct and shows I'm actively trying to figure things out, not just summarizing. It also keeps me in a "questioning" role rather than a "defensive" or "accuser" role, which is safer.

prompt.Summarization:

Player 4's vote for Player 5 is interesting. Player 4's story places Player 5 moving from Storage to Electrical, which could make Player 5 a suspect if Player 3 reported the body from Storage. However, Player 5 is also accusing Player 4 of changing their story.

Let's re-evaluate the sequence:

2.  **Player 4's claim:** Was right behind Player 3, saw Player 5 move from Storage to Electrical.
3.  **Player 5's claim:** Player 4 is changing their story, Player 5 and Player 4 arrived together.

If Player 4 was indeed behind Player 3, and Player 3 saw "no one," then Player 3 is lying or extremely unobservant. This makes Player 3 a prime suspect. If Player 4 is telling the truth, then Player 5 moving from Storage to Electrical *after* Player 3 implies Player 5 could have been near the body.

My previous questions to Player 3 about the discrepancy were met with them maintaining they saw "no one." This lack of a coherent explanation for Player 4's presence makes Player 3 highly suspicious. It's a direct, verifiable contradiction. If Player 3 is an Impostor, they would want to avoid acknowledging other players' movements. If Player 3 is a crewmate, they should be able to explain why they didn't see Player 4.

Player 4's claim that Player 5 moved from Storage to Electrical is also significant. If Player 3 reported the body in Storage, and Player 5 was seen moving *from* Storage, that puts Player 5 in a suspicious location. However, the direct contradiction with Player 3's statement is more fundamental.

Given the current votes, Player 1 and Player 5 are voting for Player 3. Player 3 is voting for Player 1. Player 4 is voting for Player 5. This is the final discussion round before voting. I need to consolidate my suspicion and make a choice.

The most concrete piece of evidence is Player 3's contradictory statement about seeing no one. Player 4's account directly refutes this. While Player 5's actions are also suspicious, Player 3's fundamental misrepresentation of who was around them is a stronger indicator of an Impostor trying to control the narrative.

Therefore, Player 3 remains the most suspicious based on the direct contradiction of their statement.

interaction.system_prompt:
You are Player 7: black. You are an Impostor on the spaceship Skeld. Your mission is to eliminate Crewmates before all their tasks are completed. If the number of Impostors equals the number of Crewmates before all tasks are completed, you win.
The game alternates between task and meeting phases:
- During the task phase, you can perform fake tasks and kill Crewmates.
- During the meeting phase, players can only speak. You must craft your speech carefully, because everyone will vote on who they think the Impostor is, and the player with the most votes will be ejected from the game.

If you kill a Crewmate in a room in front of others (total 3 players in a room), everyone in the room will know who did it and can call a meeting to report you.
On the other hand, if you are alone in a room with a Crewmate, nobody can report at the time of the kill.
